questions.q: Explain the importance of entrepreneurship in economic development. Discuss the role of entrepreneurs in promoting economic growth, creating employment opportunities, and contributing to innovation.
questions.answer: Entrepreneurship plays a vital role in economic development as it promotes economic growth, creates employment opportunities, and contributes to innovation. Entrepreneurs are individuals who take risks to start and manage their own businesses, creating new products, services, and processes that meet the needs of society. They contribute to economic growth by increasing the production of goods and services, which in turn increases the GDP of a country. Entrepreneurs also create employment opportunities for themselves and others, which helps to reduce unemployment and increase the standard of living. Furthermore, entrepreneurs contribute to innovation by introducing new technologies, products, and services that improve the quality of life and increase productivity.
marking_scheme: Importance of entrepreneurship in economic development (1 mark)
Role of entrepreneurs in promoting economic growth (1 mark)
Role of entrepreneurs in creating employment opportunities (1 mark)
Role of entrepreneurs in contributing to innovation (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the characteristics of a successful entrepreneur? Discuss the importance of these characteristics in achieving success in business.
questions.answer: A successful entrepreneur possesses certain characteristics that enable them to achieve success in business. These characteristics include a strong desire to take risks, a willingness to learn and adapt, good communication and leadership skills, and a clear vision and mission. A successful entrepreneur is also proactive, flexible, and able to think creatively and critically. They are able to identify opportunities and threats, and make informed decisions that help their business to grow and succeed. The importance of these characteristics lies in their ability to help entrepreneurs to overcome obstacles, build strong relationships with stakeholders, and make informed decisions that drive business growth and success.
marking_scheme: Characteristics of a successful entrepreneur (2 marks)
Importance of these characteristics in achieving success in business (2 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the role of small businesses in the Indian economy. Explain the benefits of setting up a small business in India.
questions.answer: Small businesses play a significant role in the Indian economy, contributing to employment generation, economic growth, and innovation. They provide opportunities for entrepreneurship, skill development, and wealth creation, particularly in rural and backward areas. The benefits of setting up a small business in India include access to a large and growing market, availability of skilled and unskilled labor, and government support through various schemes and incentives. Additionally, small businesses can take advantage of India's favorable business environment, which includes a relatively low cost of setting up and operating a business, and a range of tax and other incentives.
marking_scheme: Role of small businesses in the Indian economy (2 marks)
Benefits of setting up a small business in India (2 marks)

questions.q: A small business has a total investment of Rs. 10 lakhs and an expected return of 15%. If the business is able to achieve a return of 20%, calculate the additional profit earned by the business.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Expected return = 15% of Rs. 10 lakhs = 0.15 x 10,00,000 = Rs. 1,50,000
Actual return = 20% of Rs. 10 lakhs = 0.20 x 10,00,000 = Rs. 2,00,000
Additional profit earned = Actual return - Expected return = Rs. 2,00,000 - Rs. 1,50,000 = Rs. 50,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of additional profit earned (2 marks)

questions.q: A company has a break-even point of 500 units and a selling price of Rs. 100 per unit. If the variable cost per unit is Rs. 60 and the fixed cost is Rs. 10,000, calculate the profit earned by the company if it sells 700 units.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Break-even point = 500 units
Selling price per unit = Rs. 100
Variable cost per unit = Rs. 60
Fixed cost = Rs. 10,000
Contribution per unit = Selling price per unit - Variable cost per unit = Rs. 100 - Rs. 60 = Rs. 40
Profit = (Number of units sold - Break-even point) x Contribution per unit
= (700 - 500) x Rs. 40
= 200 x Rs. 40
= Rs. 8,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of contribution per unit (1 mark)

questions.q: A firm has a total cost of Rs. 1,50,000 and a total revenue of Rs. 2,00,000. If the firm's fixed cost is Rs. 30,000, calculate the variable cost per unit if the firm produces 1000 units.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Total cost = Rs. 1,50,000
Total revenue = Rs. 2,00,000
Fixed cost = Rs. 30,000
Variable cost = Total cost - Fixed cost = Rs. 1,50,000 - Rs. 30,000 = Rs. 1,20,000
Variable cost per unit = Variable cost / Number of units produced
= Rs. 1,20,000 / 1000
= Rs. 120
marking_scheme: Calculation of variable cost per unit (2 marks)
